mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 456321M as of 2016-08-01 reflects ongoing consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth periods do not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative growth periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate exceeding 2.0% signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when activated.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% as of 2016-08-01, well below threshold
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances, or the average 60-day return differential between high-rate and low-rate regimes is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) and payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) jointly signal robust productive capacity and labor utilization. When both series show positive momentum simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days are positive due to earnings expansion expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.4 and PAYEMS at 144598K as of 2016-08-01
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint INDPRO-PAYEMS interaction signal with 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of simultaneous positive momentum in both series do not produce significantly higher returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining by more than 10% over a 2-month window signals weakening real estate demand and forward-looking economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as a leading indicator of broader economic stress.
failure_condition: When HOUST declines > 10% in 2 months, SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the average 5-day return following such declines is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate interact such that when inflation rises above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.5%, the real interest rate becomes negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive 20-day SPX returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 239.8 and FEDFUNDS at 0.4% as of 2016-08-01
failure_condition: Rank IC of the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction with 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods meeting the trigger condition do not produce significantly higher returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerating above 200K per month signals strong labor market momentum and rising consumer purchasing power, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS monthly growth > 200K, SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances, or the average 60-day return differential between high-growth and low-growth employment periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly declining signals broad-based demand weakness and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both series show contraction simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16570.2B and RSAFS at 456321M as of 2016-08-01
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint GDPC1-RSAFS contraction signal with 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of simultaneous contraction in both series do not produce significantly lower returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).
